title: Promises, Async Functions, and the Event Loop · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Coordinate status requests, deadlines, retries, and user cancellation without races or unhandled failures. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bounded asynchronous status coordinator after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace call stack, task, microtask, rendering, and worker boundaries without treating timing as random.
Create, return, chain, and observe promises while preserving values and rejection paths.
Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Event Loop Jobs evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Event Loop Jobs assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Schedule a microtask, timer, and two fetch-like promises for request 2; abort the stale request and predict the observable log order before running it. No unhandled rejection may escape.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Event Loop Jobs has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

stimulus.title: Async Error Handling case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Schedule a microtask, timer, and two fetch-like promises for request 4; abort the stale request and predict the observable log order before running it. No unhandled rejection may escape.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A surrounding synchronous try-catch catches any later promise rejection.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle asynchronous failures at the owning boundary and prevent floating or unhandled promises. Artifact under review: a bounded asynchronous status coordinator.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
